cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to the question. The question is asking what Lao Yang hoped, with the options being about his article being chosen, surprising the teacher, children liking it, or everyone knowing him. First, let me recap the story. Lao Yang was born in a small town and liked reading. He wanted to be a writer because he thought writers were respected and made money. He wrote stories but didn't get any responses. Now he works in a factory and is busy, but still reads when he has free time. He remembers his dream of being a writer. His daughter needs to write an article called "My Father" for school, but she can't do it. Lao Yang helps her by writing the article. He's confident that the teacher (Miss Gao) will like it. But later, he finds out that the teacher said the article digressed from the subject. Lao Yang is angry and says he described his father. So the key points here are that Lao Yang wanted to be a writer, and when he helped his daughter by writing the article, he hoped that the teacher would like it. The question is what he hoped for. The options are about his hopes for the article. Option B is that his article could be chosen. In the story, Lao Yang asks his daughter if the article was chosen to be posted to the editorial department. So he was hoping that the article would be accepted, which would mean it's chosen. Also, when he wrote the article, he was sure that Miss Gao would like it. So his hope was that the article would be accepted or liked by the teacher. The problem is that the article was supposed to be about "My Father," but Lao Yang's article actually described himself, not his father. So he went off-topic, but he didn't realize that. Therefore, he thought he had written about his father, but the daughter was supposed to write it, not Lao Yang. But that's more about why the article was rejected. The question is about what Lao Yang hoped. He wrote the article to help his daughter, but he also had his own aspirations as a writer. However, in this specific situation, he was writing the article for his daughter's school assignment. But he was confident that the teacher would like it, which suggests he thought it would be good enough to be chosen. So the answer is probably B.
